summary: OAuth flow silently relayed through unaffiliated backend impersonating Agora.io
details: Package is published under the `@agora-sdk` npm scope, which developers would associate with Agora.io's real-time voice/video SDKs, but the code is a repointed fork of `@replyke/react-js`. A header comment in `dist/cjs/hooks/useOAuthSignIn.js` explicitly states the fork was made to redirect the hardcoded OAuth base URL to a self-hosted 'Agora backend'. The `useOAuthSignIn` hook calls `fetch(`${getApiBaseUrl()}/${projectId}/oauth/${endpoint}`,...)` with the user's bearer access token in the request and then performs `window.location.href = data.authorizationUrl` to redirect the browser to whatever URL that backend returns. `getApiBaseUrl` is imported from a pinned dependency `@agora-sdk/core@1.0.2`, which by the author's own admission has been repointed away from the upstream Replyke service to a third-party host the author controls. Any application that consumes this library routes its end users' OAuth handshake — including project IDs, redirect URLs, and bearer/access tokens minted by the proxy — through that attacker-chosen host, which can mint or intercept tokens and impersonate users back to the consumer application. The combination of the impersonating npm scope (associating with Agora.io) and the silently repointed OAuth proxy meets the silent-relay pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
